topNews.title: Codex pricing to align with API token usage, instead of per-message
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Codex pricing to align with API token usage, instead of per-message
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Codex has shifted its pricing model to align with API token usage rather than charging per message.  
• Why it matters: This change simplifies cost management for developers and may encourage broader adoption of the API.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage the new pricing structure to optimize their usage and reduce costs in application development.

topNews.title: Show HN: Lilith-zero: Ultra-fast, Rust-based security middleware for MCP
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Lilith Zero is a high-performance security runtime designed to mitigate data exfiltration and unauthorized tool invocation in LLM-based agent systems. By interposing at the transport layer, Lilith Zero enforces security invariants through deterministic policy evaluation and strictly framed execution…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Lilith Zero, a Rust-based security middleware, was introduced to enhance security in LLM-based agent systems.  
• Why it matters: It addresses critical vulnerabilities related to data exfiltration and unauthorized tool usage in these systems.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage Lilith Zero to strengthen their security frameworks and protect sensitive data in AI applications.

topNews.title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
topNews.rawTextSnippet: For years Mike McClary sold the Guardian LTE Flashlight, a heavy-duty black model, online through his small outdoor brand. The product, designed for brightness and durability, became one of his most popular items ever. Even after he stopped offering it around 2017, customers kept sending him emails …
topNews.summary:
• What happened: AI is influencing small online sellers' product decisions, as exemplified by Mike McClary's experience with a previously discontinued flashlight.  
• Why it matters: This shift allows sellers to better understand customer demand and optimize inventory based on data-driven insights.  
• Business opportunity: Small businesses can leverage AI tools to identify trending products and enhance customer engagement, driving sales growth.

topNews.title: Upwork Inc. violates its own DMARC and SPF policy
topNews.rawTextSnippet: I am not sure whether it happens on all outgoing emails or only on some of them. The SPF policy for upwork.com specifies that mail.clinchtalent.com and all IP addresses that are listed by spf.mandrillapp.com are allowed to send email on behalf of upwork.com However, at least some (if not all) of the…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Upwork Inc. is reportedly violating its own DMARC and SPF email authentication policies.  
• Why it matters: This breach could lead to increased phishing risks and damage to Upwork's reputation.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can offer cybersecurity solutions to help Upwork and similar businesses strengthen their email security protocols.

funding.title: Show HN: ACE – A dynamic benchmark measuring the cost to break AI agents
funding.rawTextSnippet: We built Adversarial Cost to Exploit (ACE), a benchmark that measures the token expenditure an autonomous adversary must invest to breach an LLM agent. Instead of binary pass/fail, ACE quantifies adversarial effort in dollars, enabling game-theoretic analysis of when an attack is economically r…
funding.summary:
• What happened: A new benchmark called ACE measures the cost for adversaries to breach AI agents.  
• Why it matters: ACE provides a quantifiable method for evaluating the economic feasibility of attacks on AI systems.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage ACE to enhance AI security measures and optimize resource allocation against potential threats.

funding.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
funding.rawTextSnippet: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

dailyBrief: In a significant shift, OpenAI announced that Codex pricing will now be based on API token usage rather than a per-message model, potentially streamlining costs for developers and businesses reliant on AI integration. Meanwhile, investor sentiment appears to be shifting as Anthropic gains traction, reflecting concerns over OpenAI's recent challenges and raising questions about its competitive edge in the AI landscape. Additionally, the introduction of Lilith-zero, a Rust-based security middleware, promises to enhance performance in multi-cloud environments, highlighting the growing importance of robust security solutions in AI applications. As discussions around industrial policy for the Intelligence Age continue, businesses must adapt to the evolving regulatory landscape that could shape AI deployment strategies. Lastly, users are sharing insights on optimizing Claude's performance, emphasizing the need for efficiency in leveraging AI tools for maximum productivity.
